howItWorks.intro: This 5k pace calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is a good 5K time?
faq.answer: Beginner: 30-35 min. Intermediate: 22-28 min. Advanced: 18-22 min. Elite: under 16 min. Average US race finisher: about 28 minutes.

faq.question: How do I run a faster 5K?
faq.answer: Interval training is key. Run 400m repeats at target pace with rest. Also do tempo runs at slightly slower than race pace for 20-30 minutes.

faq.question: 5K to 10K prediction?
faq.answer: Double your 5K time and add 1-3 minutes. A 25-minute 5K typically translates to a 52-53 minute 10K.

faq.question: How often can I race a 5K?
faq.answer: Every 2-4 weeks. The 5K is short enough that recovery is quick. Racing frequently is good practice for pacing and mental toughness.
